notes: Gemini CLI is the IN-PLACE rewind variant (like claudecode), not the fork variant (codex). BACKFLOW (recorded 2026-06-12, two structurally-identical runs): VERIFIED in-place — exactly ONE session_id throughout, ZERO transcript_removed, no session change, final state ready. MECHANISM CORRECTION vs the assessment: in v0.46.0 /rewind appends BOTH a {"$rewindTo":<id>} marker AND a {"$set":{"messages":[...]}} envelope that truncates the messages array to the rewound point (not just $rewindTo). The parser handles both ($set → harvest+Skip; a $rewindTo line has neither $set nor type → default Skip), so the rewind itself emits no turn — BUT the $set messages rewrite is transcript_activity, which briefly flips the session ready→working→ready. So the original 'no state_transition to working' invariant during the post-rewind window does NOT hold and was dropped; the LOAD-BEARING in-place invariant is 'no transcript_removed for primary session' + same session_id, which both hold. PICKER (driver): the RewindViewer is TWO-STAGE — it opens with the selection on 'Stay at current position' at the BOTTOM, so keys:Up selects the prior assistant message, keys:Enter opens a 'Confirm Rewind' sub-dialog, and a second keys:Enter confirms '1. Rewind conversation'. Turn boundaries are not pinned tightly: the API-key flash tier turns run long (~60s) and the rewind's $set churn adds extra working/ready cycles, so phases use generous windows and pin same_session_as session_birth rather than asserting an exact turn count. observations.model = gemini-3.5-flash (the API-key/AI-Studio tier default), not the OAuth gemini-3-flash-preview — corrected.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch (pre-session or chats sessionId, whichever arrives first)
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch (the launcher's heap-bump re-exec can delay the bind a few seconds)
The first user prompt ('one') drives the session ready → working — the turn cycle begins on the same session row
The first turn completes back to ready (flash-tier turns can run ~60s) — checkpoint to rewind back from, on the same session

Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after /rewind + Up + Enter + Enter the session is in ready on the SAME session_id, and NO transcript_removed fires (gemini rewinds in place by appending a $rewindTo marker + a $set messages rewrite the parser skips; it does NOT fork to a new session or remove the old one). The in-place invariant is the load-bearing rewind signal.

Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — the post-rewind prompt ('three') drives the SAME session ready → working (gemini rewinds in place rather than forking).
Spec: the post-rewind turn completes back to ready on the same session — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point; final live state is ready.
